milestones.title: Obtain Entry-Level Coaching Certification
milestones.description: Complete a recognised Level 1 or equivalent national coaching certification in your sport. This is the minimum requirement to coach independently.

milestones.title: Gain Practical Coaching Experience (Volunteer)
milestones.description: Log at least 100 hours of supervised coaching experience with a club, school, or community programme. Document sessions and seek feedback.

milestones.title: First Aid & Safeguarding Training
milestones.description: Complete an approved First Aid certificate and a safeguarding/child protection course. Mandatory for working with athletes of all ages.

milestones.title: Enrol in a Sports Science / Coaching Degree Programme
milestones.description: Pursue a Bachelor's degree in Sports Science, Kinesiology, or Coaching to build your theoretical foundation and expand career opportunities.

milestones.title: Obtain Advanced Coaching Licence
milestones.description: Advance to Level 2/3 or equivalent. Specialise in a sport-specific coaching pathway (e.g., UEFA B Licence, NCSA Level 3).

milestones.title: Build a Coaching Portfolio & Network
milestones.description: Document your coaching philosophy, session plans, and athlete outcomes. Attend coaching conferences and join national coaching associations.

milestones.title: Earn a Business or Sports Management Degree
milestones.description: Complete a Bachelor's in Sports Management, Business Administration, or a related field. This provides the foundational business knowledge required.

milestones.title: Complete an Internship with a Sports Organisation
milestones.description: Secure a placement with a club, sports governing body, events company, or sports marketing agency. Gain hands-on experience in operations, marketing, or partnerships.

milestones.title: Develop Core Business Skills
milestones.description: Build proficiency in project management, budgeting, sponsorship, and event logistics. Consider a PMP certification or relevant online courses.

milestones.title: Obtain Sports Management Certification
milestones.description: Pursue the SMWW (Sports Management World Wide) Certificate or a similar credential to validate your specialist knowledge.

milestones.title: Build a Sports Industry Network
milestones.description: Attend major sports business conferences (Leaders Week, Sportel, etc.), join sport business networks, and grow your LinkedIn presence.

milestones.title: Pursue an MBA or Specialist Master's
milestones.description: For senior leadership roles, consider an MBA with sports management focus or a Master's in Sport Business at an accredited institution.

milestones.title: Complete an Accredited Sport Science Degree
milestones.description: Earn a Bachelor's in Sport Science, Exercise Physiology, or Strength & Conditioning. Choose a programme with strong placement links to elite sport.

milestones.title: Gain CSCS Certification
milestones.description: Pass the NSCA CSCS examination to validate your strength & conditioning knowledge. Widely recognised in elite sport globally.

milestones.title: Placement at a Performance Centre
milestones.description: Secure a placement or internship with a professional club, national institute of sport, or Olympic / Paralympic programme.

milestones.title: Complete a Master's in High Performance Sport
milestones.description: A Master's degree (e.g., MSc Strength & Conditioning, MSc Performance Analysis) significantly accelerates access to elite roles.

milestones.title: Earn BASES Accreditation
milestones.description: Complete the BASES supervised experience scheme to gain full accreditation as a Sport & Exercise Scientist. Required for many elite positions in the UK.

milestones.title: Publish Research or Present at Conferences
milestones.description: Contribute to the evidence base by publishing applied research or presenting case studies at sport science conferences (BASES, NSCA, ACSM).

milestones.title: Complete Undergraduate Degree in Science or Medicine
milestones.description: Earn a Bachelor's in Sport Science, Exercise Physiology, Physiotherapy, or a medical degree (MBBS/MBBCh). This is the required foundation for all specialist pathways.

milestones.title: Obtain Professional Registration
milestones.description: Register with the relevant professional body: HCPC for physiotherapists/sport therapists, GMC for sport physicians. This is legally required for practice.

milestones.title: Complete Clinical Placements
milestones.description: Log mandatory supervised clinical hours across relevant settings (hospital, sport clinic, elite sport). Essential for developing practical competence.

milestones.title: Specialise in Sport Science or Sport Medicine
milestones.description: Pursue a Master's or clinical specialisation in a specific domain: biomechanics, physiology, nutrition, or sport medicine.

milestones.title: Work in a Sport or Clinical Environment
milestones.description: Gain paid experience in sport clubs, physiotherapy clinics, hospital sport departments, or national sports institutions.

milestones.title: Publish Research & Build CPD Record
milestones.description: Maintain Continuing Professional Development (CPD) log. Publish applied or clinical research to establish credibility and advance your career.

milestones.title: Obtain Level 2 Fitness Instructor Qualification
milestones.description: Complete an accredited Level 2 Gym Instructor / Fitness Instructor qualification. This is the entry-level requirement to work unsupervised in a gym.

milestones.title: Obtain Level 3 Personal Trainer Certification
milestones.description: Upgrade to a Personal Training certification to legally deliver 1-to-1 training sessions, design bespoke programmes, and conduct fitness assessments.

milestones.title: Gain First Aid Certification
milestones.description: Complete an approved Emergency First Aid at Work (EFAW) or equivalent certificate. Required by most employers and insurance providers.

milestones.title: Build a Client Base (first 10 clients)
milestones.description: Start training clients through referrals, social media, or gym floor opportunities. Focus on delivering results and collecting testimonials.

milestones.title: Develop a Specialisation
milestones.description: Choose a niche: weight management, strength training, pre/postnatal fitness, sport-specific conditioning, or senior fitness. Specialised trainers command higher rates.

milestones.title: Establish Online / Hybrid Training Business
milestones.description: Expand beyond in-person sessions by offering online coaching, digital products, or hybrid programmes. This scales your income beyond hourly limitations.

milestones.title: Earn Journalism / Media Degree or Diploma
milestones.description: Complete a Bachelor's in Journalism, Media Studies, or Communications. An NCTJ Diploma is widely respected in the UK as an alternative route.

milestones.title: Build a Content Portfolio
milestones.description: Start a sports blog, podcast, or YouTube channel. Contribute to student newspapers, local outlets, or online sports publications to build a body of work.

milestones.title: Complete Journalism Work Experience
milestones.description: Shadow journalists at local newspapers, radio stations, or sports websites. Even a week's placement can open doors and provide bylines.

milestones.title: Develop Digital & Social Media Skills
milestones.description: Learn SEO writing, video editing, social media strategy, and podcast production. Digital-first skills are essential in modern sports media.

milestones.title: Land First Paid Media Role
milestones.description: Apply for junior reporter, sports desk assistant, or content writer positions at newspapers, sports websites, broadcasting companies, or sports brands.

milestones.title: Build Personal Brand & Niche Authority
milestones.description: Establish yourself as the go-to voice on a specific sport, team, or topic. Grow your social following and pitch to larger publications and broadcasters.

milestones.title: Build Programming & Statistics Foundation
milestones.description: Learn Python and/or R to proficiency, and master fundamental statistics. These are non-negotiable skills for any analytics role.

milestones.title: Earn a Data or Analytics Degree / Certification
milestones.description: Complete a degree in Statistics, Computer Science, Mathematics, or a relevant Google/Coursera Data Analytics certificate.

milestones.title: Complete a Sports Analytics Project
milestones.description: Build a public portfolio project using real sports data (e.g., Statsbomb, NBA API). Create visualisations and publish on GitHub or a blog.

milestones.title: Learn Video & Performance Analysis Software
milestones.description: Gain proficiency in Hudl, SportsCode, Wyscout, or similar platforms used in professional sport environments.

milestones.title: Participate in Sports Analytics Competitions
milestones.description: Enter competitions like the MIT Sloan Sports Analytics Conference research paper contest, Kaggle sports challenges, or Big Data Bowl.

milestones.title: Land an Analytics Role in Sport
milestones.description: Target entry-level analyst roles at clubs, sports data companies (Opta, StatsBomb, Second Spectrum), betting/media firms, or national governing bodies.

milestones.title: Earn Nutrition or Dietetics Degree
milestones.description: Complete a Bachelor's in Nutrition & Dietetics, Nutritional Science, or Sport Science with a nutrition focus. This is required for SENr registration.

milestones.title: Obtain SENr or Precision Nutrition Certification
milestones.description: Register with the Sport and Exercise Nutrition Register (SENr) in the UK or complete Precision Nutrition Level 1 & 2 for an internationally recognised credential.

milestones.title: Complete Supervised Placement in Sport
milestones.description: Undertake a supervised placement with a sport club, national team, or sport nutrition practice. Document cases and receive mentorship.

milestones.title: Learn Body Composition Assessment (ISAK)
milestones.description: Complete ISAK Level 1 accreditation to be able to conduct body composition measurements — a core competency in elite sport nutrition.

milestones.title: Build Supplement & Doping Regulation Knowledge
milestones.description: Develop in-depth knowledge of the WADA prohibited list and supplement risk management to advise athletes safely at elite level.

milestones.title: Build a Client / Team Portfolio
milestones.description: Document your successful interventions with athletes and teams. Seek referrals, contribute to athlete education, and begin publishing applied practice articles.

resources.name: Journal of the International Society of Sports Nutrition

milestones.title: Complete a PE or Sport Science Degree
milestones.description: Earn a Bachelor's in Physical Education, Sport Science, or a related degree. This is the foundation for all teaching qualification routes.

milestones.title: Complete a PGCE or School-Based Training Route
milestones.description: Earn Qualified Teacher Status (QTS) through a PGCE, School Direct, or Teach First programme. This is mandatory to teach in maintained schools.

milestones.title: Complete Safeguarding & DBS Checks
milestones.description: Obtain Enhanced DBS (Disclosure and Barring Service) clearance and complete mandatory safeguarding training. Required before working with young people.

milestones.title: Complete Newly Qualified Teacher (NQT) Induction
milestones.description: Successfully complete the NQT (now Early Career Teacher) two-year induction period with professional support and structured mentoring.

milestones.title: Lead Extra-Curricular Sport Programme
milestones.description: Run school sports teams, fixtures, and events. Taking on additional responsibilities demonstrates leadership and boosts progression to Head of PE.

milestones.title: Pursue Continuous Professional Development
milestones.description: Complete CPD in inclusive education, coaching qualifications, assessment innovation, or leadership. Required for progression to senior roles.

milestones.title: Complete Psychology Undergraduate Degree (BPS Accredited)
milestones.description: Earn a BPS-accredited Bachelor's in Psychology. This is the required entry point for all professional sport psychology pathways in the UK.

milestones.title: Earn a Master's in Sport Psychology
milestones.description: Complete an MSc in Sport & Exercise Psychology from a BASES or BPS-accredited programme. This is required for HCPC registration and BASES accreditation routes.

milestones.title: Complete BASES Supervised Experience Scheme
milestones.description: Log a minimum of 500 hours of supervised applied practice to gain BASES Sport & Exercise Scientist accreditation in the psychology division.

milestones.title: Develop Core Mental Performance Tools
milestones.description: Master key psychological techniques: goal-setting, imagery, attentional control, pre-performance routines, confidence building, and team cohesion strategies.

milestones.title: Gain Experience with Athletic Populations
milestones.description: Work with sport clubs, schools, or performance centres to gain supervised applied experience with real athletes across different competitive levels.

milestones.title: Obtain Chartered Status or CMPC Credential
milestones.description: Pursue BPS Chartered Psychologist status or the AASP Certified Mental Performance Consultant (CMPC) credential for full professional recognition.

milestones.title: Earn an LLB Law Degree or Graduate Diploma in Law
milestones.description: Complete a law undergraduate degree or the GDL (Graduate Diploma in Law) as a conversion route. This is the foundational requirement for all legal pathways.

milestones.title: Complete Legal Practice Course (LPC) or Bar Course (BPTC)
milestones.description: Depending on solicitor or barrister track, complete the LPC (now SQE) or Bar Professional Training Course. These lead to qualification to practise.

milestones.title: Specialise in Sports Law via LLM or CPD
milestones.description: Pursue an LLM in Sports Law or complete targeted CPD in sports contracts, doping regulation, CAS arbitration, and governing body rules.

milestones.title: Gain Experience at a Sports Law Firm or Governing Body
milestones.description: Secure a training contract or internship with a sports law firm, sports governing body (FIFA, FA, World Athletics), or sports agency.

milestones.title: Study WADA & Anti-Doping Regulations
milestones.description: Develop detailed knowledge of the World Anti-Doping Code, WADA prohibited list, therapeutic use exemptions, and CAS jurisprudence.

resources.name: CAS — Court of Arbitration for Sport

milestones.title: Build Profile Through Sports Law Networks
milestones.description: Join the British Association for Sport and Law (BASL), attend sports law conferences, publish articles in sport law journals, and cultivate relationships across the sports industry.

resources.name: BASL — British Association for Sport and Law
